new_text: [zerobrain] ZeroBrain Project: Custom Agent Zero fork inspired by Torus. Agent Zero v0.9.8.2 has monologue loop, JSON-in-text tools, 24 built-in tools, FAISS memory (all-MiniLM-L6-v2, 384d), 3 areas (MAIN/FRAGMENTS/SOLUTIONS), similarity 0.7, token budget = ctx_length*0.7, cascade compression, 24 lifecycle hooks, NO safety gates, multi-agent parent‚Üíchild hierarchy, LiteLLM routing, MCP client, A2A protocol, Docker isolation. Key: extensions are injection point for gate-like enforcement. Torus has 17 active gates (1-7, 9-11, 13-19); Agent Zero has ZERO. tool_execute_before hook is natural integration point for gate enforcement.

ARXIV: SAGE - Self-evolving Agents (arXiv:2409.00872). Combines self-reflection + memory augmentation. Agents reflect on past, store distilled knowledge, retrieve to improve future performance. 2.26X improvement closed-source, 57.7%‚Äì100% open-source, large gains on smaller models. Torus applicability: validates memory-first+causal chain. Smaller models gain proportionally more from gate logic. Implementable: Add post-task reflection hook. After session_end, compare (a) what memory said fix would be vs (b) what actually worked (fix_outcomes). If discrepancy>threshold, generate correction entry with type:correction tag. Closes loop: Memory‚ÜíAct‚ÜíReflect‚ÜíUpdate Memory.

ARXIV: Agent-R (arXiv:2501.11425) & Multi-Agent Reflexion (MAR, arXiv:2512.20845). Agent-R: iterative self-training, on-the-fly reflection, prevents error loops, +5.59% baseline. MAR: extends to multi-agent with diverse personas + judge model synthesizing critiques; acting/diagnosing/critiquing/aggregating. HotPotQA +3, HumanEval +6.2. Torus applicability: Agent-R prevents Gate 6 deadlock. MAR's separation maps to agent/gate/mentor. Implementable: When gate 6 fires repeatedly (deadlock), spawn reflection sub-agent diagnosing WHY. Store diagnosis to LanceDB with type:error. Reset gate6_warn_count only if diagnosis identifies genuine fix, not bypass.

ARXIV: JitRL (arXiv:2601.18510) & Lifelong Learning Roadmap (arXiv:2501.07278). JitRL: training-free, frozen LLMs adapt at test-time via logit optimization, gradient-free, continual learning without updates. Lifelong Roadmap: 3 pillars‚ÄîPerception (multimodal), Memory (evolving store), Action (grounded). Core challenge: catastrophic forgetting. Torus: gates run inference-time; JitRL means gates adjust dynamically based on session signal without retraining (threshold warm: tight early, relax as track record improves). Memory Module exists (LanceDB); missing: catastrophic forgetting prevention. Implementable: preserve_score field in LanceDB. High scores survive compaction. Score increases on successful retrieval/use. Compactor deletes preserve_score<threshold AND age>max_age only.

HyMem Research (arxiv:2602.13933, Feb 2026): Hybrid Memory Architecture with Dynamic Retrieval Scheduling. Dual-granular storage: L1 (event-level summaries: context/time/location/participants) + L2 (raw dialogue). Explicit L1‚ÜíL2 links. Two-tier: (1) Lightweight‚Äîcosine L1, low latency. (2) Deep‚Äîconditional, ~22% activation. Coarse semantic recall ‚Üí LLM self-retrieval ‚Üí L2 backtrack. Reflection: completeness check, decompose to sub-problems. Benchmarks (gpt-4o-mini): LOCOMO 89.55% vs Full 77.51% vs Zep 75.14%; multi-hop 88.16% vs 46.89%; token cost 1.5k vs 21.4k (92.6% reduction). LongMemEval-s: 75.00% vs LightMem 68.64%. Weakness: no write-time consolidation, completeness choke point, no cross-session evolution.

HyMem (arxiv:2602.13933, Feb 2026): Dual-granularity (summary/raw, NOT episodic/semantic). L1: summaries (context, time, participants); L2: raw text (full fidelity). Two-tier retrieval: T1 (lightweight, cosine L1, completeness eval); T2 (deep, conditional, ~22% activation, coarse recall ‚Üí LLM self-retrieval ‚Üí L2 backtrack, decomposes unresolved to sub-queries). Benchmarks (gpt-4o-mini): LOCOMO 89.55% (multi-hop 88.16% vs 46.89% full), single-hop 85.15%, temporal 92.98%, token 1.5k vs 21.4k (92.6% reduction). LongMemEval-s 75.00%. Strengths: SOTA, 92.6% reduction, elegant backtracking. Weaknesses: no write-time consolidation, completeness bottleneck, no cross-session evolution, misses semantic abstraction.

Toroidal Teams v1 live test (session 410): 2 agents (researcher-alpha/beta) parallel dispatch for dual-layer memory papers. Bugs: (1) Watcher tmux paste-Enter race‚Äîtext sits unsubmitted, fix: add delay/poll. (2) idle_prompt_hook overwrites detailed results‚Äîclobbers data, fix: agents write detailed to ~/.claude/data/research/<topic>-<agent>.json, receipt is lightweight (status/summary/memory_ids/path). (3) status file stuck at 'stopped', fix: manual reset. Applied: created data/research dir, updated agent protocols (step 5=detailed to data/research, step 6=lightweight receipt), created researcher-beta. TODO: fix paste-Enter race (sleep 0.5), fix launch.sh status reset, skip result if recent.

Session 241: GitHub-ready Torus copy at ~/Desktop/torus-github/. Key: (1) sync.sh idempotent copy with gen_sync.py avoiding auto-format corruption. (2) Excluded personal files. (3) Template configs (config.example.json, mcp.example.json, telegram). (4) Audit found 'crab' username in 6 files via project slug‚Äîfixed with dynamic computation: `_claude_dir.replace("/","-").replace(".","-")`. (5) Added deps (sentence-transformers, faster-whisper, web). (6) README.md Quick Start. (7) Pushed to github.com/OZmasterAI/Torus-Framework (private). (8) Amended+force-pushed to erase leaked username. Final: 246 files, 1 clean commit 5ce1c40.

Future Gate 20 extensions: C ‚Äî Trigger-specific question. After A+2Q fires, append 1 question specific to triggering risk signal (edit_streak‚Üíintent, Write‚Üímechanical, pending_verification‚Üíroot-cause). ~10 lines, ~80 tokens/session. D ‚Äî PostToolUse reflection gate. After Edit/Write: "Just wrote [file]. Complete: 'This change does X to solve Y.' If can't complete cleanly, reconsider." Intent-only, non-blocking, ~600 tokens/session, new file + settings.json entry. Build order: Gate 20‚Üíobserve‚Üíadd C if needed‚Üíadd D if needed. All additive, non-breaking.

[zerobrain #2] ZeroTor gate upgrade (13‚Üí16) verified. Present: gate_07_loop_escape, gate_08_depth_limit, gate_20_token_budget, gate_21_critical_files, gate_22_canary; extensions monologue_start/20_torus_loop_reset.py, tool_execute_after/25_torus_output_scan.py; tier 5+10+1=16. G13‚Üísystem_path_guard, G01/G05 removed. Deviations: numbering plan G20-24, agent used G07/G08 (filling holes)+G20/21/22 (logical); G04 timeout 15min‚Üí20min (better for autonomous); loop reset bonus. Unverified: 227 test claim. Gate tests: test_gates.py=59, test_new_gates.py=30, test_phase3_gates.py=43, test_enforcer.py=15.

[zerobrain] ZeroTor corrections: (1) G13‚âÝG02: G02=destructive shells, G13=Python writes. Complementary. (2) safety_critical_files‚âÝG13: G13 guards system (/etc, ~/.ssh); safety_critical guards app (.env, Dockerfile, .pem). ZeroTor missing both. (3) G06 dormancy correct design (causal chain IS workflow). (4) Output scanner underrated: G17 scans inputs‚Üíweb; output scanner scans results‚Üêweb. Different attack vector. (5) G05 vs G19 not redundant: G05 proactive, G19 reactive. G05 regex noisy in single-tool; salvageable with better signal (consecutive code+no test output). Recommendations: Remove G01/G05, rename G13, loosen G04 to 15-20min, add Loop Escape/Depth Limit/Token Budget/Safety Critical/Output Scanner=16 gates.

Branch comparison (2026-03-09): Cognitive vs github-branch. github-branch HEAD=ea5524411d (v2.7.0, 2026-02-26); Cognitive HEAD=693d2c19 (gate-tuning+metrics, 2026-03-09). Cognitive from mem2x+combo2+5 commits. Additions: (1) metrics_collector in enforcer.py; (2) G05 BLOCK_THRESHOLD 3‚Üí4; (3) G14 PENDING_THRESHOLD=2+overrides; (4) G06 float guard (state.py 441-444) + dict guard (gate_06:60); (5) test.pass_rate in orchestrator.py; (6) mem2x modules (memory_decay, ltp_tracker, knowledge_graph, entity_extraction, memory_replay) on Cognitive only; G16 ruff+AST reverted‚Üíidentical to github-branch. settings.json identical. Both have gate_16_code_quality.py.

Session 402 (Cognitive): SuperClaude comparison + 3 upgrades. Gate 16: added nesting depth (warn‚â•4, escalate‚â•7) + function length (warn‚â•80, escalate‚â•150) AST checks, fail-open, tunable. Nesting 6‚Üí7 (legit gate code), length 60‚Üí80/100‚Üí150. Gate 20 (new): risk-scored conditional firing. Signals: edit_streak‚â•3(+2), Write(+1), pending_verification‚â•2(+1), no test+framework(+1), G16 warned(+2). Fires‚â•3, 2 Q's (mechanical+intent), non-blocking. ~3-4/session, ~120 tokens. Extensions C/D deferred. Analysis: Four Q's (SuperClaude) behavioral; Gate 4+Gate 20 cover same mechanically. A+2Q beats alternatives (88.1 score, 120 tokens vs 300-900).

5 SuperClaude features to adopt: (1) Research depth tiers‚Äî--depth quick|standard|deep|exhaustive to /research skill, ~50 tokens. (2) Pre-impl confidence gate‚Äîtest baseline+clear target before Edit, extend Gate 4/new Gate 14. (3) Hop-pattern vocabulary‚Äîadd patterns (entity expansion, temporal progression, conceptual deepening, causal chains) to /research SKILL.md, ~100 tokens. (4) Confidence-aware search‚Äî>0.5 use directly, 0.2-0.5 verify, <0.2 unknown, ~30 tokens in CLAUDE.md. (5) PDCA tags‚Äîtype:pdca-plan/do/check/act with feature: prefix. Total increase: ~30 tokens (only #4 in CLAUDE.md).

Session 27 Sprint 5 (v2.0.6): 7 improvements. Smart Gates: (1) Gate 5 progressive scoring (full test=100, targeted=70, script=50, generic=10, clear‚â•70). (2) Gate 6 escalation to BLOCKING after 5 warnings (resets on remember_this). (3) Gate 9 retry budget‚Äîactive_bans dict with fail_count (allow=1, warn=2, block=3+, successful get +1). Memory: (4) Tag co-occurrence matrix lazy-built, auto-expands queries (>40% threshold), results tagged. New Skills: (5) /profile‚Äîtooling, baseline, hotspots, optimize, metrics. (6) /analyze-errors‚Äîdeep query, timeline, root cause, correlate fix_history, prevention, playbook. Tests: (7) 16 new for v2.0.4+v2.0.5. Total: 17 skills, 588 tests, 19 MCP tools, memory_server.py 75KB.
